sourceMovie.title: Flushed Away
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Flushed Away for the same reason it broke through in 2006: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a animation rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. David Bowers keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The adventure and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Moana
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Flushed Away clicked for you, Moana (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and animation, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Flushed Away?
faq.answer: Try Moana, Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa, One Hundred and One Dalmatians—each shares DNA with Flushed Away while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Flushed Away?
faq.answer: Flushed Away (2006) is best described as Adventure, Animation, Comedy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Flushed Away worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
